Digital Twin Applications in EPC Projects
A. Samad, R. Wasnik, H. Singh, and F. Kamal, National Petroleum Construction Company, Musaffah, Abu Dhabi,
U.A.E.

Abstract
Engineering drawings are the most critical project documentation, which provides processing plant
blueprint. Relational database and attributes driven graphics are being used to transform CAD drawing to
intelligent drawings. These intelligent drawings and design data is used to make smart decisions. The latest
methods to create project digital twin are evolving to make use of these intelligent drawings. This paper
discusses the user friendly and innovative solutions to accomplish digital twin goals at EPC stage.
P&ID is a valuable data source for oil and gas projects. It is used to generate pipelines, instrumentations
and equipment lists. These are mainly used for material take-off and other procurement purposes. In
Intelligent P&IDs these lists reporting may be customized and attained, automatically. During digital twin
creation process, sharing of data with different departments via direct links and data lake is necessitated.
During project EPC stage, data lake may be used to find many out of the box solutions e.g., training AI
models to predict project completion dates and material supply-demand issues, proactively.
Due to stringent project schedules, it is always challenging to achieve an important milestone of
timely issuance of Intelligent P&IDs. Variety of difficulties during development of intelligent P&IDs are
summarized below:
1. Drafting of modification and demolition P&IDs for brown-field project
2. Drafting of colour coded system turnover P&IDs
3. Revision & scope clouds Customization to meet digital twin requirements methods in available
intelligent software are not user friendly.
Many steps are involved to perform above mentioned tasks. Various lists generated from P&IDs
such as line list consumes significant time and prone to error if not managed properly. Digital twin
requirements make it further complex. Various techniques are used to perform routine drafting of P&IDs,
attain required reporting and digital twin targets. However, these need to be managed and customized by
highly skilled professionals. There is a need for tailor-made solutions in the intelligent P&ID software to
make operations user friendly. Cloud computing big data handling capability and AI may help to meet digital
twin requirements. Archiving and retrieving data from ultra-fast cloud facilities may solve many problems.

SPE-216770-MS
This paper provides ideas to debottleneck various limitations in the currently available drafting software.
It is to add more intelligence and smooth interconnectivity among different disciplines thus making them
more user friendly and smart. This all may translate in speeding up the drafting and error free reporting
with the help of AI. It may enhance software capabilities beyond simple drafting and reporting to meet new
era requirements of digital twin.
Evolution of CAD (Computer Aided Design) and Digital Twin
There are many types and kinds of engineering drawings e.g., plant schematics, logical diagrams (P&IDs,
PFDs, MSDs and electrical / instrumentation diagrams), orthographic projections drawings (scaled and
dimensioned layouts, plot plans, alignment sheets, fabrication cut lists, hoppers and ducts development
drawings, mechanical assemblies and related detailed drawings), isometric and cavalier projection drawings.
CAD (Computer Aided Design) software has been in the market for a long time, but after 1990 it
started replacing conventional drafting boards and machines, very speedily. The emergence of PCs (Personal
Computers) and GUI (Graphical User Interface) atmosphere has also played a major role in giving rise to
CAD software. Based on GUI philosophy, Windows and Macintosh operating systems have a major role
in making PCs user friendly and easy to operate. Word processing and calculation spreadsheet software
indirectly helped CAD users to get better and more accurate results, robustly. CROs (Central Record Offices)
within any EPC or engineering design company required a large physical space to store drawings in an old
Victorian era library setup. Modern computer hard disk drives and dedicated servers helped to store a very
large quantity of drawings and documents in a very small space. This all helped us to design and manage
relatively larger projects than ever before.
Twenty first century started with the great influence of computers in our daily lives as well as in the
energy sector. PCs started playing major roles in all spheres of professional lives due to the enhancements
in computer hardware systems, improved GUI OSs (Operating Systems), rise of easy computer coding
languages and the advent of internet. This all also has opened the doors for smart CAD (2D and 3D
both). RDB (Relational Database) and attributes driven 2D and 3D graphics are being used to create smart
drawings. Smart CAD helped to build and manage further big processing plants in the energy sector.
Processing plant sizes are getting bigger day by day and it is equally multiplying and ballooning design
and engineering activities. Local servers and hard disks are getting replaced by hi-class cloud services
to handle the emerging demand of robust computing and bigger disk space for computing, archiving and
retrieval. In today's corporate world, the CPUs (Central Processing Units) and TBs (terabytes) disk space is
insufficient to do the said tasks nicely. Scalable, reliable, secure, efficient, cost effective and fault tolerant
cloud services are easily available now. GPUs (Graphics Processing Units) or TPUs (Tensor Processing
Units) and PTs (petabytes) large disk space is available on demand. More computing power and theoretically
infinite storage space is available now to handle big data (filled with drawings, documents and structured
or unstructured information). In pure financial and business terms, it has significantly cut COPEX (Capital
Expenditures) and OPEX (Operating Expenditures) costs. Time span has reduced to minutes from days to
deploy these tools.
Now, methods to create project digital twin are evolving. In a multidisciplinary design and engineering
atmosphere, RDB, data driven graphics and cloud computing and storage powers are helping to integrate
and analyze inputs, outputs, and results to achieve digital twin goals. Fusion of AI and Smart CADDING
is the new front to achieve further big and great.
All engineering drawings and documents are important, but P&ID has a primary role during this whole
activity, which provides foundations to design, construct and operate a processing plant. One of the main
purposes of digital twin is to build a real-world processing plant's digital replica. P&ID remains the main
document during this exercise. It is P&ID which gives directions to piping department to develop accurate
3D models and prepare MTO (Material Takeoff reports). The Instrumentation department is bound to get

SPE-216770-MS
3
directions from P&ID to develop its detailed drawings. Mechanical department fetch important design data
of equipment from P&IDs to move forward. Commissioning related testing is not possible without systems
turnover P&IDs in hand. In short, from concept to operations, running plant surveys, cost calculations and
plant brown filed revamping activities we need P&ID as a basic document.
In this paper we are critically analyzing available P&ID applications limitations and their role in digital
twin model. This paper is primarily and critically analyzing smart P&IDs role in digital twin applications,
but collective achievements and fears supported with concrete recommendations are also shared for the
subject cause i.e., Digital Twin Applications in EPC Projects.
How digital twin applications may play their role during any processing plants average life of 30 to 50
years? It is a big question which demands answers with concrete recommendations and solutions. This paper
has critically analyzed this issue also and has proposed some recommendations to forestall this futuristic
problem in a more plausible and preemptive style.
Smart P&ID Role in Digital Twin
During the pre-digital twin era, smart drawings and 3D models have produced remarkable results. Still,
we are getting good results, but most of the time those results were compiled and stacked in individual
siloes. Unique tagging conventions for piping components, instruments, pipelines, and equipment are used
throughout the project (from design till operation). These are not specific to individual disciplines e.g.,
process, piping, instrument or mechanical but application to whole project. Somewhat quasi-centralized
interdisciplinary integration worked well during smart CADDING era, but now it is strongly realized
to centralize the flow of unique tags to streamline multidisciplinary EPC activities. It may help to save
manhours by avoiding repeated tasking, duplicity detection and may improve overall project management
and performance.
After enumerating some accomplishments, this paper is highlighting software limitations and attributes
driven graphics preservations issues in the future.
Achievements
Digital twin platform is launched successfully. This is helping EPC contractors as well as esteemed clients.
There are several applications involved to accomplish the goal, but the paper's primary focus is on Smart
P&IDs. Data is flowing from P&ID to multiple directions to benefit all involved. Through XML export, our
digital twin team has integrated different vendors utilities output to get roust and better results for digital
twin model.

6:
6
SPE-216770-MS
limitations have been observed in available smart P&ID software. We will highlight a few in the paper.
P&ID is a main and primary document in any processing plant:
1.
Brown field P&IDs (construction and demolition P&IDs) handling is not easy and user-friendly task
in available smart P&ID software. Graphics and data extraction both have lacunas and discrepancies,
which need to be mitigated. Demolition and construction P&IDs names are normally the same.
a. How to keep 02 files with same name in same database together? Available smart P&ID software
are unable to address this issue wisely.
b. Demolition P&ID is dumb and construction P&ID is smart. Digital Twin demands maximum
stuff available on same platform not in siloes. Demolition VS Construction P&IDs case directly
challenges this objective. Available smart P&ID software are unable to address this issue wisely.
2.
During brown field project engineering, EPC companies are bound to their scope area only. EPC
companies control / identify their scope area with scope clouds mostly. Smart P&ID software is
aesthetically and intelligently very poor to address this issue.
3.
Line list is a main report extracted from smart P&ID software. We get results, but the process is very
cumbersome, awkward and not user-friendly. Intelligent line list should be able to include / exclude
future, new, revamped and vendors scopes by itself and automatically.
4.
Instrument list is a very critical document, which inherit data from P&ID for multiple disciplines use
and even for operations It is utilized by instrumentation, construction, commissioning and operations
departments on different stages to accomplish their tasks, respectively. For example, during drafting
a short detailed graphics of MOV, PCV & FCV etc. are shown in the P&IDs. Comprehensive detail is
implied and hidden somewhere in invisible space, but it is available in legends. The instrumentation
list should carry whole details of each implicitly or explicitly available instrument. Only an admin
with high level smart P&ID admin skill may configure required reporting system, which is not good.
Software Enhancement Proposals
Any smart P&ID admin with years of working experience, IT skills & dexterity, handful process engineering
knowledge and understanding of P&ID may only configure an excellent reporting system and mechanism.
Proposals are listed below to handle above stated issues:
1.
To render a good and realistic digital twin model, demolished and constructed areas details (both 2D
and 3D) during plant life cycle should be available at the disposal of simple buttons clicks. It will
help operating companies to conduct several studies successfully in the proceeding years of plant
operation and its life.
2.
Revision and scope clouds are not up to the mark in available smart P&IDs software. May times they
overlap or form an awkward shape, once drawn. Drawing them in smart P&IDs software is not quick
or time efficient.
a. These should intelligently be able to tag encircled areas with revision / scope area information.
Clouded areas and encircled elements should automatically fall under drawn cloud category e.g.
revision number or scope area definitions and limitation.
b. A drawing or diagram should be very neat and clean with eye catching standardized looks
controlled under ISO, DIN or ASME standards. Therefore, aesthetically these should be nice
enough to pass through required flanges and Tie-In points.
c. There is a need to feel the importance of revision / scope clouds. Once we feel the importance then
a mutually decided and well consented revision and scope clouding system may be designed.
3.
A reliable and intelligently extracted line list needs excellent configuration as well as a very good
team of process designers. During drafting if main / primary lines are mixed up with branches, vent

7:
SPE-216770-MS
7
and drain lines then it is next to impossible to extract reliable line list. Such drafting practice invites
multifold re-checking and bug fixing activities. Software vendors ought to feel the importance of this
element as well and provide tailor-made solutions. Few proposals are listed below:
a. Different operating companies have different templates for line, instrument, equipment and valves
reporting. Smart P&ID software vendor may provide ready-made templates based on inputs from
big client companies.
b. There should be provisions to restrict drafting teams within certain parameters under the umbrella
of already set policies and rules. For example, if a branch is tapped out of a pipeline and is not
given a separate line number then its priority should be set to secondary, automatically by smart
P&ID software. Likewise, vent and drain lines should automatically be identified by the smart
P&ID software to keep their identity separate and unique.
c. If the same line type is used to draw vent or drain lines which are tapping out of a main line, then
the system should restrict the designer to do so.
4.
After consultation with different operating companies some project templates may be configured and
placed within smart P&ID setup files directories. It will help to install the software and smoothly
map the operating company's specific requirements. It will produce seamless results with minimum
configuration.
However, all above may be configured in all available smart P&ID software, but only an excellent smart
P&ID admin can configure it. There is a need that smart P&ID software vendors to feel the importance of
reporting systems for any processing plant. Finally, this all will serve as a digital twin model. Previously
plant design had outputs and results in siloes and were loosely integrated for specific departments, but now
digital twin requirements demand hi-level integration and centralization. Tailor-made solutions should be
provided. These will make drafting easy and data integration and flow easy and straightforward.
Digital Twin and AI
Next milestone is AI deployment on any digital twin model. Once real production starts then future
output predictions may be further fine-tuned after deploying AI regression, classification or cluster models.
Furthermore, several studies with the help of AI may be conducted on digital twin models of any processing
plant
1.
Life span calculations of the processing plant.
2.
Product (slurries, liquid or gas) composition elements study and analysis based on forecasted and real
output.
3.
Certain piping components, instrument and equipment replacement and upgradation requirements.
4.
Safety studies may be done in digital atmosphere; provided live plant data is available in digital twin
model.
All EPC project documents and drawings are available in digital format. With the help of modern digital
techniques, these documents and drawings (2D diagrams, orthographic projection and 3D models) are
available together in a single platform i.e., digital twin.
As mentioned earlier, AI may help to achieve certain goals ranging from EPC project execution to
processing plant life span. AI supervised, un-supervised or reinforcement models are data hungry and need
data. Data may easily be taken from digital documents or drawings (2D and 3D models).
Forestalling Data Loss / Inaccessibility Issues in Future
Real life processing plants life span is around 03 to 05 decades. Experience witnesses that digital assets are
prone to inaccessibility after 02 to 03 decades.

8:
8
SPE-216770-MS
Severally, it is observed that it is impossible many times to access a 10 year old digital file. Same is
applicable to digital twin.
1. If digital twin software vendor is out of business within next 02 decades, then what is the fate of
digital twin model?
2. During the project life span much upgradation, modifications, demolition and revamping is done.
How to reflect the changes in the digital twins, from time to time?
3. If a new software vendor jumps in with some excellent solutions, then how to retrieve / migrate
legacy digital twin in the new setup?
4. Processing plant documents, drawings and all digital assets including digital twin model will be in
a very bad situation if supporting software is out of the market due to any unforeseen reason.
5. AI is data hungry but proprietary rights driven digital twin models and applications will not provide
an easy access to its whole system. Proprietary products codes and internal structure are mostly
encrypted or visible in machine language only. Without specific software, adopters, patches or
plug-ins accessing data from them is next to impossible. Data utilization from EPC documents is
relatively easy because we can channelize or neutralize documents data with TXT, CSV or XML
file formats, but neutralizing data from 2D and 3D models is very cumbersome and all available
CAD software has not any common file formatting system to address this issue.
As of now, any digital file, systems or database is bound to be accessed via some proprietary software.
During digital twin creation several software vendor applications are used and then data from them is
tunneled via gateways, adaptors or plug-ins to neutralize the data under certain ISO standards and then in
the end we again pass same to a proprietary software i.e. digital twin.
Proposed Solutions
CFIHOS (Capital Facilities Information Handover Specifications), ISO 8601 and ASCII-based format
(American Standard Code for Information) ISO 10303 standards may be strengthened, involved and
implemented more forcefully to make 2D and 3D data exchange more neutral and accessible via simple
text editors even.
1.
To deal with 2D and 3D CAD data DXF and STEP are open-source file formats available to everybody
under GNU or Apache licenses (General Public License).
2.
Similar to DXF or STEP or better file formats may be developed or explored to make 2D and 3D
data exchange possible in more neutral formats, which should be human as well as machine-readable
formats.
3.
Any proprietary digital twin software or related applications should be legally and professionally
bound by global industrial and software communities and conglomerates to enable their software to
produce above discussed ASCII-based neutral files at any required time.
4.
Above stated ASCII-based neutral files may also be updated / generated parallel to normal save-as
or export / import options.
Above will forestall most discussed issues in a more amicable way. No one is a loser in this proposed
plan. It is a win-win situation for everybody e.g., operating companies, EPC contractors, end users and
software vendors.
1. At the end client company is the owner of data. Therefore, his ownership should be true ownership
without any strings attached.
2. Client should be free to jump to any other better digital twin platform or applications, whenever
they want or find better options in the future.

9:

3. Any old or new vendor may jump in and help esteemed clients migrate their existing digital twin
model and related applications data seamlessly into a new, more robust, cost-effective, efficient,
secure, fault-proof and better platform easily.
4. It will open the door for healthy competition to develop better digital twin applications and
technology in the future.
5. Most of the time, AI needs ASCII-based data to train its image processing, NLP (Natural Language
Processing) or tabular data related supervised, un-supervised or reinforcement models.
6. So far, data in an ACSII-based file format is a promising data migration / utilization format for any
AI models training. More plugins and adopter's involvement to get neutralized files will obviously
generate messy and unclean data. Garbage-in and garbage-out is the primary principle to get a good
AI trained model for accurate forecasting and analysis.
The above stated proposals will pave a way for excellent data acquisition to train good AI models.
Conclusion
In any digital twin model P&ID importance is evident therefore upgraded smart P&ID software, which
is considering P&ID importance better than before from all aspects and tackle brown field P&IDs issues
e.g., demolition and construction P&IDs nicely in any processing plant database have multifold benefits.
In the later stages of any running processing plant when revamping is required, then revision and scope
clouds intelligent entry in the smart P&IDs system and tailor-made solutions for reporting system carry
more benefits for digital twin goals as well as for any AI model training.
EPC projects execution range life from 03 to 05 years, but on other end processing plant real life span
range from 30 to 50 years. There is a need to work on concrete footings to evolve digital twin models and
applications.
1. Digital twin life span should not be haunted by the ghost of mortality e.g., inaccessibility after 10
or more years or unavailable data links to get required data easily from related applications for any
kind of AI models training.
2. Proposed parallel ASCII-based data saving / transfer mechanism is the need of hour to make digital
twin model and applications immortal and available at any time and for anybody easily.
AI is the talk of the day and next milestone in the foreseeable future. Processing plants may autonomously
operate, deliver live and real data with the help of AI driven robots, systems and available cloud based
massive computing and processing power. There is a need to take concrete steps towards technological
progress, wisely and futuristically.
